channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Mali insurgents attack multiple towns and a prison, army says
item.description: Insurgents in ​Mali staged attacks in at least five locations ‌early ⁠on ‌Saturday, including at a prison in Kenieroba, according to an army statement. The fighting comes more than two months after the country's defence minister was killed.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 15 million people expected to attend former supreme leader's funeral
item.description: Mourners thronged a vast prayer complex in ​Tehran on Saturday as the week-long funeral ceremonies of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began with the national anthem, religious eulogies and readings from the Koran. 15 million people are expected to attend the former leader's funeral, according to Iranian authorities, FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah said, reporting from Tehran, adding that the event would take place also in neighbouring Iraq.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pope Leo's visits to Lampedusa on July 4 'symbolic'
item.description: Pope Leo XIV, who has sparred with the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, spent the Fourth of July on Saturday in the epicenter of Europe’s migration debate to honor the tens of thousands of people who have died trying to reach Europe to find freedom and prosperity. While the United States marks the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence with rallies, parties and fireworks, history’s first US-born pope traveled to the Sicilian island of Lampedusa to pray at a migrant cemetery and celebrate a solemn Mass for the island's newest arrivals. FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Foreign rescue workers help with recovering Venezuela's quake victims
item.description: With little hope of finding loved ones alive, frustrated families of those affected by Venezuela's double earthquakes clamored for help to recover their loved one's bodies Friday as uncertainty grew over the death toll from the tragedy. Authorities on Friday reported a total of 2,645 deaths and more than 12,000 wounded from the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes that struck the country last week, with most fatalities in the coastal town of La Guaira, where scores of residential complexes were flattened.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump marks US 250th celebration with speech and Mount Rushmore flyover
item.description: US President Donald ​Trump called on Americans to protect the freedoms the nation's founders envisioned 250 years ago against what he has portrayed as the "communist" threat posed by progressive Democrats, speaking on the eve of Independence Day at Mount Rushmore.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: England face Mexico in Azteca fortress, returning to scene of Maradona handball
item.description: Thomas Tuchel’s stuttering England are an ideal target for co-hosts Mexico as they seek a prestigious World Cup win in their final game at the near-impregnable Azteca stadium, the scene of the infamous Maradona handball that still haunts the Three Lions decades later.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin DODMAN

item.title: 250 years of US independence: How France helped turn the tide of the Revolutionary War
item.description: After months of discreet support, France officially allied itself with the American revolutionaries in their war against the British Empire, a decision that would permanently alter the fate of both nations.
item.dc:creator: Barbara GABEL

item.title: US 'royal wedding': Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce marry at Madison Square Garden
item.description: US pop queen Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce officially tied the knot on Friday, triggering frenzied global attention around America's "royal wedding". The couple made no public announcement about their wedding plans and reportedly insisted that guests sign non-disclosure agreements.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pope defends migrants on symbolic visit to key Mediterranean hub Lampedusa
item.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Saturday on Italy's Lampedusa island, a major port of call for migrants who risked the perilous crossing from North Africa, in a symbolic rebuke to the anti-immigrant rhetoric coming from US and EU political factions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump hails American exceptionalism before railing at communism at 250th celebration
item.description: US President Donald Trump ushered in the 250th anniversary of US independence on Friday with rhetoric about American exceptionalism before calling on the country to fight what he called the sinister threat of communism. The speech evoked the Red Scare of the 1950s, one of the country’s ugliest chapters, when alleged communists were persecuted and blacklisted from jobs across America.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East live: Iran begins days-long funeral for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran on Saturday began a days-long funeral for the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The 86-year-old was killed in an air strike at the start of the US-Israeli war with Iran. Mourners wept when authorities unveiled the casket containing Khamenei’s body at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia secure last 16 spot with narrow victory over Ghana
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Colombia secure last 16 spot with narrow victory over Ghana
item.description: Colombia booked their place in the last 16 with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Ghana in Kansas City. Jhon Arias' early goal proved decisive as the Cafeteros dominated proceedings but failed to add to their advantage, ultimately holding on through Ghana's second-half pressure. Colombia will take on Switzerland on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Colombia-Ghana: Arias fires Cafeteros past Black Stars
item.description: Colombia secured the final last 16 berth at the World Cup 2026 with a 1-0 victory over Ghana in Kansas City, claiming the last remaining Round of 16 spot. The Cafeteros' hard-fought triumph sees them advance to face Switzerland next Tuesday. Relive our liveblog to see how things unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina survive epic Cape Verde scare after extra-time thriller
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina survive epic Cape Verde scare after extra-time thriller
item.description: Argentina eliminated a heroic Cape Verde side 3-2 in an extraordinary Round of 32 encounter at Miami after a pulsating extra-time period that tested the defending champions to their limit. The Albiceleste's resilience was matched only by the Blue Shaks' refusal to surrender, with the match swinging dramatically multiple times before Cristian Romero's 111th-minute header sealed Argentina's passage to the last 16.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: More than 400 dead in DR Congo's Ebola outbreak
item.description: In tonight's edition, Ebola is spreading beyond its original hotspot in the DRC as a new case has been detected nearly 600 kilometers away. And another human rights catastrophe is unfolding in Sudan around the besieged city of al-Obeid, the UN says.
item.dc:creator: Clarisse FORTUNÉ

item.title: World Cup 2026: Salah's Egypt beat Australia in penalty shootout to reach last 16
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Salah's Egypt edge Australia on penalties to reach last 16
item.description: Mohamed Salah coolly tucked away a panenka penalty as Egypt beat Australia 4-2 in a penalty shootout to clinch their first win in a World Cup knockout match, after their round of 32 match finished even at 1-1. They will face either Argentina or Cape Verde in the last 16.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: England-Mexico World Cup match could be moved earlier due to storm risk
item.description: The round of 16 World Cup match between England and Mexico could be moved forward to noon local time on Sunday due to a risk of storms and flodding near Mexico City's Aztecaon stadium, local media reported. Fifa has yet to confirm the time change.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Putin & The ‘Drowning Man Syndrome’ Sudan
item.description: From intensifying drone warfare between Ukraine and Russia to mounting fears of a humanitarian catastrophe in Sudan, anti-immigration protests in South Africa and a landmark ruling by the US Supreme Court, this week has been shaped by conflicts over power, identity and international law.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: 'Not a proxy war': Sudan conflict 'would not go on like this' without international support
item.description: Pospisil says the siege has been ongoing for almost a year, with the RSF appearing determined to seize el-Obeid. While he argues that Sudan's civil war is not a proxy war in the strict sense, he also makes clear that "without international support, the war would not go on like this." Yet none of the external actors involved, whether the UAE, which supplies arms to the RSF, or the US and Saudi Arabia, has the leverage to bring the warring parties to a ceasefire, let alone a negotiated settlement.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE

item.title: Pope calls for 'moderation' in US public discourse on 250th anniversary of independence
item.description: Pope Leo, the first US-born pontiff, called for "moderation" in US public discourse and called on Americans to find "common ground" in a speech on Friday that coincided with the 250th anniversary of US independence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Iran gets ready for Khamenei's funeral
item.description: He was assassinated in a #US-Israeli strike in February 2026. #Iran's Supreme leader Ali #Khamenei's #funerals are set to be held on July 4, with authorities expecting between 15 and 20 million people to attend.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
